title: Stomata gas exchange
script_summary: A concise explanation of stomata gas exchange that intrigues and informs.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you tiny openings on leaves control life itself?
These openings are called stomata, essential for gas exchange.
When they're open, they let in carbon dioxide and release oxygen.
But how do they know when to open or close? It all depends on light and moisture.
Without stomata, plants can't photosynthesize, and life as we know it would be impossible.
Want to explore more science mysteries? Hit that follow button!
